sections.paragraph:
to cut off, to circumcise (comp. מָהַל, מָלַל No. II., נָמַל No. I.); with an acc. of pers. Gen 21:4 Exo 12:44 Jos 5:4, Jos 5:7and of the foreskin, Gen 17:23. Metaph. Deu 10:16, וּמַלְתֶּם אֵת עָרְלַת לְבַבְכֶם “circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart,” remove impure things from your mind [?]; Deu 30:6 comp. περιτομὴ καρδιάς, Rom 2:29, and Arab. طهر to circumcise, prop. to purge, because the foreskin was regarded as unclean and profane.

Niphal, to be circumcised, to circumcise oneself, Gen 17:10, 13 Gen 17:13 34:15 , 17 Gen 34:17, 22 Gen 34:22. Figuratively, Jer 4:4, הִמֹּלוּ לַיהֹוָה “circumcise yourselves to Jehovah,” i.e. put away all wickedness from your minds, and consecrate yourselves to Jehovah.

Pilel מֹולֵל to cut down. Impers. Psa 90:6, לָעֶרֶב יְמֹולֵל וְיָבֵשׁ “in the evening it is cut down (man like grass) and withereth.”

Hiphil, to cut off, destroy (a people), Psa 118:10, 11 Psa 118:11, 12 Psa 118:12.

Hithpalel הִתְמֹולֵל to be cut off at the point, blunted (as arrows); Psa 58:8, יִדְרֹךְ חִצָּו כְּמֹו יִתְמֹלָֽלוּ “(where) he sends his arrows, (they shall be) as if cut off at the point,” i.e. blunted, good for nothing.

Derivative, מוּלָה.
